meta.description: High-signal items only: contracts, earnings, funding, IPO signals, defense awards, acquisitions. urgency: immediate=act now, same_day=review today, watch=monitor

immediate.title: Air Force Vice Chief: Demand for New Airplanes ‘Outstripping’ Production
immediate.summary: The Air Force is seeking to buy around 108 new aircraft in fiscal 2027—though really, officials wish they could get more. The issue isn’t just more money, according to the service’s No. 2 officer; contractors just aren't able to produce enough aircraft at the present moment, Gen. John Lamontagne said June 4 at AFA’s Mitchell Institute for Aerospace Studies. The post Air Force Vice Chief: Demand for New Airplanes ‘Outstripping’ Production appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: GE, Rolls-Royce Get Contracts to Advance Autonomous Drone Engine Designs
immediate.summary: The Air Force has awarded contracts to General Electric Aerospace and Rolls-Royce to further hone their designs for engines that would power medium-thrust autonomous drones. The post GE, Rolls-Royce Get Contracts to Advance Autonomous Drone Engine Designs appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: Muon Space Debuts High-Power ‘Ultra’ Satellite Designed for Orbital Data Centers
immediate.summary: Muon Space debuted a new, higher power satellite platform on Wednesday, targeting orbital data centers as an key application. The first pathfinder mission is planned for delivery in 2028. Condor-Ultra [] The post Muon Space Debuts High-Power ‘Ultra’ Satellite Designed for Orbital Data Centers appeared first on Via Satellite .

immediate.title: NDAA Would Cut SDA, RCO Offices
immediate.summary: The House’s annual defense authorization bill would eliminate the two offices within the Pentagon designed to speed up space acquisition. The post NDAA Would Cut SDA, RCO Offices appeared first on Payload .

immediate.title: Now Is the Time to Boost CCA Investment
immediate.summary: The Air Force wants about $1 billion to move Collaborative Combat Aircraft into production in fiscal 2027 and accelerate the introduction of this game-changing technology. Congress should support that objective. The post Now Is the Time to Boost CCA Investment appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: Air Force Seeks New Gearboxes for CV-22 in ’27 Budget
immediate.summary: The Air Force plans to spend $213 million on its CV-22 fleet in fiscal 2027, about one-fifth of a $1.12 billion investment in the tiltrotor aircraft across the Pentagon. The post Air Force Seeks New Gearboxes for CV-22 in 27 Budget appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: NASA Drains 66-Million-Gallon Reservoir to Upgrade Critical Water System
immediate.summary: A powerful but mostly unseen water system at work during rocket engine tests at NASA’s Stennis Space Center near Bay St. Louis, Mississippi, underwent an upgrade in May. Crews brought the High Pressure Industrial Water Facility’s 66-million-gallon reservoir to its lowest level since construction in the 1960s by pumping out about 40 million gallons of []

immediate.title: Blue Origin rocket explosion shows ‘fragility’ of national-security launch plans
immediate.summary: Space Force efforts to breed more competitors aren’t keeping up with ever-increasing demand for rockets.

immediate.title: Lawmakers Push Space Force to Seek More Commercial Integration
immediate.summary: House lawmakers want the Space Force to buy more technology and services from commercial companies, evidenced in a handful of amendments approved as part of the House Armed Services Committee’s version of the fiscal 2027 National Defense Authorization Act. The post Lawmakers Push Space Force to Seek More Commercial Integration appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: House Panel Votes to Prohibit F-22 Retirements Through Fiscal 2032
immediate.summary: The Air Force would keep all of its 184 F-22 Raptors until at least the end of fiscal 2032 under an amendment added to the House Armed Services Committee’s version of the National Defense Authorization bill. The post House Panel Votes to Prohibit F-22 Retirements Through Fiscal 2032 appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: Glittering star cluster image reveals missing patch of stars: 'We were not looking for the gap, but we found it'
immediate.summary: Euclid space telescope observations of ancient globular cluster NGC 6397 reveal a subtle gap in brightness distribution of red dwarf stars.

immediate.title: KC-46’s New Remote Vision System Completes Initial ‘Non-Contact’ Testing
immediate.summary: Boeing has completed initial flight testing on its redesigned Remote Vision System for the KC-46 tanker, the defense giant announced June 4. The new system, dubbed RVS 2.0, is meant to replace the original camera and video system boom operators use to refuel other aircraft, which has faced issues for years now. The post KC-46’s New Remote Vision System Completes Initial ‘Non-Contact’ Testing appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

In the mission named after [] The post Infinite Orbits and Open Cosmos Plan ‘Tom Jerry’ Space Rendezvous Mission appeared first on Via Satellite .

Mbryoncis is a photonics company based in Ireland that develops optical [] The post Mbryonics and NTT to Collaborate on Optical Communications Infrastructure appeared first on Via Satellite .

immediate.summary: More than 50 new Starlink satellites are now in orbit after launches from California and Florida.

immediate.title: US Reduces Forces Committed to NATO, Tells Allies to Step Up Air and Naval Power
immediate.summary: The U.S. is reducing the number of forces it is prepared to commit to NATO in a crisis, U.S. European Command said in a June 3 announcement. The changes, which were previewed to alliance members last month by a senior Pentagon official, appear to include a decrease in the number of combat aircraft, air-to-air refueling planes, and Navy vessels. The post US Reduces Forces Committed to NATO, Tells Allies to Step Up Air and Naval Power appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: New Push for Separate ‘Cyber Force’ Builds, but Questions Remain
immediate.summary: The idea to establish a new military service dedicated to cyber warfare reared its head again this week thanks to a proposal in Congress and a think tank report. But essential questions about how a so-called “Cyber Force” would be manned and work with the other services remain. The post New Push for Separate Cyber Force Builds, but Questions Remain appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.summary: Expert tester rated the Celestron NexStar 8SE telescope with an impressive review and it's now at its best price of the year in this early Amazon Prime Day deal.

immediate.title: China launches debut mission of Falcon 9-like rocket with no advance notice (video)
immediate.summary: China launched its partially reusable Long March 12B rocket for the first time ever on Monday (June 1), sending it up without issuing airspace closure notices ahead of time.

Citizen scientist Gary Eason made this image using raw data from the JunoCam instrument, applying digital processing techniques to enhance color and clarity. It provides a detailed view of chaotic clouds []

same_day.title: Registration open for Aerospace Test & Development Show 2026
same_day.summary: Registration for the Aerospace Test & Development Show 2026 at Hall 7, MEETT, Toulouse, is now open. The show takes place on 29–30 September 2026. The Aerospace Test & Development Show is the worlds leading and largest event 100% dedicated to aircraft, drone, helicopter, space and defence testing and development, and is organised by the publishers of Aerospace Testing International. This years edition will be the most comprehensive to date. With more than 150 exhibitors expected and over 70 speakers confirmed, the 2026 show will feature three free-to-attend forum stages: the Testing Forum, the Defence Testing Forum, and the Future Propulsion Forum — giving attendees more opportunities than e

same_day.title: How new FAA rules are opening the skies to personal eVTOLs
same_day.summary: Imagine operating a personal VTOL from your driveway and transitioning seamlessly into open skies. With the FAA’s Modernization of Special Airworthiness Certification ruling, the dream of taking off from your home, avoiding traffic and flying directly to your destination is a step closer to reality. The MOSAIC rule changes how VTOLs can be certified for personal use. The ruling, finalized in July last year, removes older maximum weight limits for Light Sport Aircraft (LSA) in favor of performance-based metrics, paving the way for owner-operated VTOLs. Continue reading How new FAA rules are opening the skies to personal eVTOLs at Aerospace Testing International.

same_day.title: Impulse Space Closes $500M Series D
same_day.summary: “Often in a fundraise like this, there’s some narrative of, ‘Okay, well, now we move into this new line of business, or now we go build the factory in Ohio,’” President and COO Eric Romo told Payload. “There’s none of that here. This is really about, ‘We need more of the same. We need to continue on the same trajectory we’re on.’” The post Impulse Space Closes $500M Series D appeared first on Payload .
